display.purpose: Hero headline only. Maximum compression at 900 weight.

headline.purpose: Section h2 headings. Tight but not as compressed as display.

title.purpose: Card and step titles. Weight-driven hierarchy at body scale.

body.purpose: Section copy, feature descriptions. Max 65ch line length.

label.purpose: Section eyebrows, phone labels. Uppercase, 2px tracking. Budget: 1 per section.

mono.purpose: Technical callouts. JetBrains Mono 500. Sparingly used.

shadows.name: device-frame
shadows.purpose: iPhone device frames only. Never apply to UI containers.

shadows.name: cta-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary CTA button only. Warm anchor in the dark field.

shadows.name: light-card
shadows.purpose: Cards in the light-background features section only.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for hover and state transitions.

motion.name: stagger-1
motion.purpose: First element in a staggered reveal.

motion.name: stagger-2
motion.purpose: Second element in a staggered reveal.

motion.name: stagger-3
motion.purpose: Third element in a staggered reveal.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Override for prefers-reduced-motion. All durations collapse to effectively instant.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Collapse nav links, simplify stats grid.

breakpoints.name: desktop
breakpoints.purpose: Switch split sections from single column to 2-column grid.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main CTA. Pill shape, Cine Orange, CTA glow. Used for hero CTA and waitlist submit.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #E8822A; color: #fff; padding: 15px 30px; border-radius: 50px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; text-decoration: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 6px 28px rgba(232,130,42,0.35); transition: opacity 0.2s, transform 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.87; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #E8822A; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary CTA. Pill shape, transparent with visible border. Paired with Primary in hero.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: transparent; color: rgba(245,245,247,0.5); padding: 14px 26px; border-radius: 50px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; text-decoration: none; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.25); color: #f5f5f7; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f5f5f7; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Status Pill — IN
components.description: Gear available. Green tinted background with green text. One of four fixed semantic status variants.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-block; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 50px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.3px; } .ds-pill-green { background: rgba(48,209,88,0.16); color: #30D158; } .ds-pill-orange { background: rgba(232,130,42,0.13); color: #E8822A; } .ds-pill-red { background: rgba(255,69,58,0.13); color: #FF453A; } .ds-pill-purple { background: rgba(191,90,242,0.16); color: #BF5AF2; }

components.name: Feature Card
components.description: Feature grid card on dark background. Flat surface, subtle border, hover lift.
components.html:
QR Check-Out
Scan any item label to check it out or in — no manual lookup required.
components.css: .ds-feat-card { background: #17171c; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border-radius: 18px; padding: 26px; transition: transform 0.2s; cursor: pointer; } .ds-feat-card:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); } .ds-feat-bar { width: 4px; height: 32px; border-radius: 2px; margin-bottom: 16px; } .ds-feat-title { font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; color: #f5f5f7; margin-bottom: 7px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-feat-desc { font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.65; color: rgba(245,245,247,0.5); font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; }

components.name: Email Input
components.description: Waitlist email form input. Pill shape, surface background, orange focus border.
components.css: .ds-email-input { flex: 1; min-width: 200px; padding: 14px 18px; border-radius: 50px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); background: #17171c; color: #f5f5f7; font-size: 15px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .ds-email-input::placeholder { color: rgba(245,245,247,0.5); } .ds-email-input:focus { border-color: #E8822A; }

components.name: Navigation
components.description: Fixed nav bar. Frosted glass dark background, logo mark + wordmark, links + CTA.
components.html:
CC
CrewCase
FeaturesJoin Waitlist
components.css: .ds-nav { position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 100; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 14px 40px; background: rgba(8,8,11,0.9); backdrop-filter: blur(24px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(24px); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); } .ds-nav-logo { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: -0.4px; color: #f5f5f7; text-decoration: none; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-logo-mark { width: 32px; height: 32px; background: #E8822A; border-radius: 8px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 900; color: #fff; letter-spacing: -0.5px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-right { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 14px; } .ds-nav-link { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: rgba(245,245,247,0.5); text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #f5f5f7; } .ds-btn-nav { background: #E8822A; color: #fff; padding: 9px 20px; border-radius: 50px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; text-decoration: none; transition: opacity 0.2s; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-btn-nav:hover { opacity: 0.85; }

narrative.overview:
A camera report is the daily log that a film crew fills out to account for every roll, every mag, every piece of equipment that touched the camera. It is precise, complete, and non-negotiable. That is the sensibility of this design system: every element earns its place on the page the same way every line earns its place on a report. No decoration for decoration's sake. No color that doesn't carry information.

The system is built for the dark — deep near-black backgrounds that mirror the color temperature of a set, with one warm accent (film orange) that signals action. Status information is carried by a tight, iOS-native color vocabulary (green/orange/red/purple) that crews already know from their phones. Typography is heavy and compressed at display sizes, then relaxes into readable Inter for body copy. The experience should feel like picking up an industry tool you've never used before and immediately understanding it.

This system explicitly rejects: generic SaaS productivity aesthetics, consumer lifestyle softness, and enterprise coldness. It also rejects the current wave of film brand cliches: grain overlays without purpose, fake film stock filters, serif-on-black as a shorthand for cinematic.
keyCharacteristics: Deep dark background with near-zero lightness (#08080b) — not charcoal, not slate, void
Single warm accent (orange #E8822A) carried sparingly; its rarity is the signal
Status expressed entirely through a four-color semantic vocabulary, never through shape alone
Typography compressed hard at display sizes; Inter at maximum weight (-2.5px tracking at 900)
Surfaces layer in discrete steps: bg-void → bg-raised → surface → surface-high
Motion is functional — scroll reveals and hover lifts only; no choreography for its own sake

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Cine Orange appears on ≤2 interactive elements visible at any time. Its presence is the signal; dilute it and the signal is lost.

rules.name: The Status Contract
rules.body: Green = available, Orange = checked out, Red = overdue, Purple = maintenance. These four roles are fixed. Do not use these colors outside their status context.

rules.name: The Compression Rule
rules.body: Display type must be at weight 900 and letter-spacing ≤ -2px. Dropping to 700 or removing negative tracking at display sizes makes the page feel like a template.

rules.name: The Eyebrow Budget
rules.body: One uppercase tracked label (sect-eyebrow) per section maximum. If a section needs a label, use the eyebrow or the title — not both.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Dark surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows exist only on the device frame and the primary CTA. Everything else earns depth through tonal layering.

dos: Do use #E8822A for CTAs, active states, and the logo mark
Do keep display type at weight 900, letter-spacing ≤ -2px
Do use tonal layering (bg-void → bg-raised → surface) for depth on dark sections
Do use the four-status color vocabulary (green/orange/red/purple) strictly for gear status
Do present every app screenshot inside an iPhone device frame
Do cap section eyebrows to one per section
Do maintain prefers-reduced-motion support on all transitions
Do use border-subtle (7% white) on dark card borders
donts: Don't use a light or cream background as the page default
Don't add box-shadows to dark-theme cards or containers
Don't use Cine Orange on more than one or two elements visible simultaneously
Don't use the status colors (green, red, purple) outside their semantic status contexts
Don't design for generic SaaS aesthetics: no light neutrals as the page base
Don't design for consumer lifestyle softness: no pastel tints, no bubbly rounded corners everywhere
Don't use numbered section markers (01/02/03) as eyebrows on every section
Don't show app screenshots without a device frame
